case_title: SMILEDIRECTCLUB, INC. ET AL VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SMILEDIRECTCLUB, INC., SDC FINANCIAL, LLC, SMILEDIRECTCLUB, LLC, JEFFREY SULITZER DMD PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO SEAL RECORDS LODGED JULY 17, 2020. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SEAL RECORDS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay, Allow Limited Discovery And To Continue Hearing; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Cal .Civ. Pro. Code 425.16)
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Nicholas Secco (312-212-4949; nsecco@beneschlaw.com) for Plaintiffs.
Anthony J. Dutra (415-995-5141; ADutra@hansonbridgett.com) and John Cu (JCu@hansonbridgett.com) for Defendant.

not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs SMILEDIRECTCLUB, INC., SDC FINANCIAL, LLC, SMILEDIRECTCLUB, LLC, JEFFREY SULITZER DMD PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION's Motion To Lift Stay, Allow Limited Discovery And To Continue Hearing; (Cal .Civ. Pro. Code 425.16) is denied.

Defendant contends that Plaintiffs' motion is untimely. While Plaintiffs waited more than 30 days after Defendant filed its special motion to strike to file their motion, the Court cannot conclude that the motion is untimely as a matter of law, and will decide it on its merits. (Cf. Braun v. Chronicle Publishing Co. (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 1036, 1052 [affirming trial court's denial of plaintiff's oral discovery request at hearing on special motion to strike as untimely where plaintiff failed to file a noticed motion supported by a showing of good cause as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16(g)].)

Plaintiffs seek to discover documents from Defendant including: (1) claims or preapproval requests for Plaintiffs' aligners since July 1, 2019; (2) the organizational structure of Defendant and its affiliates; and (3) Defendant's internal policies. However, Plaintiffs fail to explain how the broad discovery they seek could have any bearing on the timeliness of Plaintiffs' claims based on Defendant's publication of an article, which is the basis for the Court's ruling granting Defendant's special motion to strike in part. Accordingly, the Court denies Plaintiffs' motion for failure to show good cause. (See Murray v. Tran (2020) 55 Cal.App.5th 10, 16 ["[t]o establish good cause, the plaintiff must . . . [identify] the specific discovery sought and [show] this discovery is needed to establish a prima facie case and tailored to that end."], quoting Abir Cohen Treyzon Salo, LLP v. Lahiji (2019) 40 Cal.App.5th 882, 891; Tutor-Saliba Corp. v. Herrera (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 604, 618 [court was "well within its discretion in denying discovery" where plaintiff's showing was "irrelevant to the issue under consideration"]; Garment Workers Center v. Superior Court (2004) 117 Cal.App.4th 1156, 1162-1163 [trial court abused its discretion in allowing depositions on issue of malice "before determining whether there was a reasonable probability the court would ever reach that issue"; if other issues can be established without discovery, "the court should consider resolving those issues before permitting what may otherwise turn out to be unnecessary, expensive and burdensome discovery proceedings"].)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
